page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter in September: Weather, Tips & Best Boats

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella yacht charter in September with our weather guide, sea temperature & top boat picks. Book a luxury boat today.
data.summary: Discover September weather conditions for a Marbella yacht charter, including sea temperatures, wind patterns, and top boat recommendations for a perfect day on the Costa del Sol.
key_facts: Average air temperature: 25–30°C with 10–11 hours of sunshine.
Prevailing wind: Poniente (west) light breeze, sea state calm.
Peak season ends mid-September; better availability and lower prices from late September.
Popular destinations: Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance and VAT.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749 for 2 hours.
Tier B Mangusta 80 (24m) minimum 4 hours at €4,719 includes a jet ski.

faq.q: Is September a good month for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, September is excellent. The weather remains warm (25–30°C), the sea is calm, and the water temperature is 22–24°C. Crowds thin after mid-month, so you get more space and often better rates.

faq.q: What is the sea temperature in Marbella in September?
faq.a: The Mediterranean off Marbella averages 22–24°C in September, ideal for swimming, snorkelling, and water toys. It stays warm well into the evening.

faq.q: Do I need to book a yacht charter in September early?
faq.a: Early September is still busy, so booking 1–2 weeks ahead is wise. From mid-September onward, last-minute availability improves, but popular boats like the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94 still require advance booking.

faq.q: What kind of boat is best for September in Marbella?
faq.a: A flybridge or open cruiser is ideal. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers shade and sun. For a sportier ride, the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) is a great choice. Both depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Are there any local events in Marbella in September?
faq.a: Yes, the Marbella International Film Festival and various food fairs happen in September. Check local listings. The harbour at Puerto Banús remains lively with restaurants and bars.

faq.q: What is the wind like in September for sailing?
faq.a: September typically has light westerly (Poniente) winds of 5–10 knots, making for calm seas. Ideal for motor yachts and comfortable for guests prone to seasickness.

faq.q: Can we anchor and swim in September?
faq.a: Absolutely. Popular anchorages like Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coast near Cabopino offer clear, warm water. All our charters include a licensed skipper who knows the best spots.

faq.q: What is included in a Marbella yacht charter with you?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

data.body_html:
September is one of the finest months for a Marbella yacht charter in September. The summer crowds thin, the sea stays warm at 22–24°C, and the air temperature hovers between 25–30°C. With 10–11 hours of sunshine and calm seas, it's the perfect window to explore the Costa del Sol by boat. Whether you want a fast speedboat or a luxury flybridge, our fleet departs from Puerto Banús and covers Marbella, Estepona, Cabopino, and Sotogrande.

September Weather in Marbella: What to Expect

September offers near-perfect conditions. Average highs reach 30°C, lows drop to 19°C, and rainfall is minimal (around 15mm for the month). The sea temperature peaks at 24°C in early September, gradually cooling to 22°C by month's end. Winds are light—typically a poniente (west) breeze of 5–10 knots—so the sea state is calm, ideal for all boat types. You'll need sunscreen (SPF 50+), a hat, and maybe a light jacket for the evening return. The sun sets around 20:30, giving you long afternoons on the water.

Best Boats for a September Charter

Our fleet offers options for every group size and budget. For small groups, the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) are fun and affordable. For a luxury experience, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) in white or grey includes a free jet ski. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) provides shaded upper deck and spacious saloon. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.

Where to Go: Top Destinations from Puerto Banús

From our base in Puerto Banús, you can head west to Estepona (8 NM), with its charming old town and anchorages like Cala del Faro. Eastward, Cabopino (6 NM) offers a quiet dune-backed beach. For a longer cruise, Sotogrande (15 NM) is a glamorous marina with golf and fine dining. The iconic Marbella Marina and Golden Mile are visible from the water. Your skipper will tailor the itinerary to your preferences.

What's Included in Your Charter

Every booking includes: a licensed English-speaking skipper, fuel for the day, bottled water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks (chips, olives, nuts). Also covered: insurance, safety equipment (life jackets, flares, first aid), and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). No surprise charges. You can bring your own food, but drinks are provided. If you want a catered lunch, we can arrange that on request.

Booking Tips for September

Early September is still peak season—book at least 1–2 weeks ahead. After mid-September, availability opens up, and you may find last-minute deals. For the best value, consider a Tier A boat (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) at €749 for 2 hours, perfect for a short afternoon cruise. For a full day (8 hours), these same boats cost €2,299. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 and includes a jet ski—great for thrill-seekers. Contact us via WhatsApp for other boats' pricing.

What to Wear and Bring

Pack swimwear, a towel, sunscreen, sunglasses, and a hat. A light jumper or windbreaker is useful for the return trip as the breeze picks up. Non-slip shoes are recommended. We provide all drinks and snacks, but you're welcome to bring your own food. Don't forget your camera—the views of La Concha mountain and the Marbella coastline are stunning.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is September a good month for a yacht charter in Marbella?

Yes, September is excellent. The weather remains warm (25–30°C), the sea is calm, and the water temperature is 22–24°C. Crowds thin after mid-month, so you get more space and often better rates.

What is the sea temperature in Marbella in September?

The Mediterranean off Marbella averages 22–24°C in September, ideal for swimming, snorkelling, and water toys. It stays warm well into the evening.

Do I need to book a yacht charter in September early?

Early September is still busy, so booking 1–2 weeks ahead is wise. From mid-September onward, last-minute availability improves, but popular boats like the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94 still require advance booking.

What kind of boat is best for September in Marbella?

A flybridge or open cruiser is ideal. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers shade and sun. For a sportier ride, the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) is a great choice. Both depart from Puerto Banús.

Are there any local events in Marbella in September?

Yes, the Marbella International Film Festival and various food fairs happen in September. Check local listings. The harbour at Puerto Banús remains lively with restaurants and bars.

What is the wind like in September for sailing?

September typically has light westerly (Poniente) winds of 5–10 knots, making for calm seas. Ideal for motor yachts and comfortable for guests prone to seasickness.

Can we anchor and swim in September?

Absolutely. Popular anchorages like Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coast near Cabopino offer clear, warm water. All our charters include a licensed skipper who knows the best spots.

What is included in a Marbella yacht charter with you?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

Ready to experience the best of Marbella in September? Browse our full fleet and book your perfect day on the water. For personalised advice, message us on WhatsApp—we're here to help.
